pattern: Count occurrences of character c; each pair (including same position) contributes one valid substring, giving count*(count+1)/2.
hints: A valid substring starts and ends with c; if c appears at positions i and j (i <= j), the substring s[i..j] qualifies.
Let m be the number of positions where s[pos] == c; we need to choose start and end positions from these m positions (with repetition when i == j for single-char substrings).
The answer is m*(m+1)/2: choose 2 from m with replacement (ordered pairs i <= j), which counts all pairs plus m single-character substrings.

edge_inputs.description: char appears only once, only one substring
large count overflow check with long all-same string

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase[:4]) for _ in range(size))
    c = rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase[:4])
    return [s, c]
